title: Update Website Banner
description:
Domain: theexteriorcompany.com


Primary URL: https://theexteriorcompany.com/


Summary: The Exterior Company is requesting an update to the current homepage banner. The existing banner text and link need to be replaced with new storm date messaging, a new destination link, and the banner must scroll on mobile.


Client Goal: The homepage banner displays the updated storm date text, links to the schedule page, and scrolls correctly on mobile devices.


Requirements for Completion:

  • Banner text is updated to read exactly: "Franklin Cranberry Storm Date 4/15/2026 | Malvern, Pennsylvania Banner Storm Date 5/20/2026 -> BOOK STORM INSPECTION"
  • Banner link is updated from https://theexteriorcompany.com/?p=66031&preview=true to https://theexteriorcompany.com/schedule/
  • Banner scrolls correctly on mobile

title: Create new landing page - Mechanicsburg
description:
Seeking help create a landing page for their new location please.


Domain: theexteriorcompany.com

Primary URL: https://theexteriorcompany.com/

Summary: The Exterior Company is opening a new Mechanicsburg, PA location and needs it added to the website footer. The client has provided the exact address, phone number, and location card details to be used.

Client Goal: The Mechanicsburg location is represented in the website footer with the correct address and phone number, consistent with how other locations are displayed.

Requirements for Completion:

  • Mechanicsburg location is added to the website footer
  • Footer entry displays the following information exactly as provided:
    • Mechanicsburg
    • The Exterior Company
    • 5275 E Trindle Rd., Suite 205B, Mechanicsburg, PA 17050
    • (717) 610-0720
  • Formatting is consistent with existing location entries in the footer

The site footer task has been created separately. I will create one too for the GBP and Citations creation.


Thanks much!

title: Add GBP Post to Lancaster GBP
description:
Hi, guys!

A big storm is hitting in PA and the client wants to post a GBP post to their Lancaster GBP highlighting it.

Here's the relevant blog post: https://theexteriorcompany.com/blog/franklin-and-cranberry-pa-storms/

Image to use:



Copy:

Storms moved across Venango County this weekend with thunderstorms and wind gusts up to 23 mph along the Allegheny River corridor. The damage isn't always obvious from the curb. Wind can break the sealant strips that hold shingles flat, hail can knock loose the granules that protect your shingles from UV, and gutters can clog with debris that backs water up onto your roof deck. 

Free roof inspections this week for Franklin, Cranberry, Oil City, and surrounding Venango County homes.

title: Add script - Roof Calculator for Website
description:
Seeking help add this script on the site please.
https://theexteriorcompany.com/

Please do let us know if there are any concerns for this one. We can coordinate w/ their AM.

Client context: Could you have your team work on getting the following code embedded into our website? I'm very sure we can start to optimize for some new keywords and advertise instant pricing then. Open to ideas on where this should go - whether its own landing page, or embedded throughout. Just want to get this up and running!

Script to add:
    <!-- Add this HTML block to the body of your webpage to display the Instant Roofer Website Calculator: -->

    <div>
      <iframe
          src="https://book.instantroofer.com/?product=estimate&id=40804b91-62ad-4871-97c9-c8ed9784b270&materials=shingleAsphaltArchitect,shingleAsphaltDesigner,metalRoof"
          style="display:block;aspect-ratio:0.53;width:345px;inline-size:345px;height:650px;block-size:650px;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;position:static;align-content:normal;align-items:normal;align-self:auto;bottom:auto;box-sizing:content-box;clear:none;clip:auto;display:block;flex-basis:auto;flex-direction:row;flex-grow:0;flex-shrink:1;flex-wrap:nowrap;float:none;justify-content:normal;left:auto;max-block-size:none;max-height:none;max-inline-size:none;max-width:none;min-block-size:0px;min-height:0px;min-inline-size:0px;min-width:0px;overflow-x:clip;overflow-y:clip;padding-bottom:0px;padding-left:0px;padding-right:0px;padding-top:0px;resize:none;right:auto;top:auto;visibility:visible;z-index:auto;border:none;"
        ></iframe>
        <div style="text-align: center; margin-top: 10px;">
          Powered by <a href="https://www.instantroofer.com">Instant Roofer</a>
        </div>
    </div>
   
    <!--
    IMPORTANT:
    If you want to record the landing page URL and/or Google Tag Manager data in your leads,
    you must also add this script to the end of the body of every page your visitors may land on
    before navigating to the page(s) containing the Instant Roofer Website Calculator.
    -->

    <script>
      window.IR_APP_ORIGIN="https://book.instantroofer.com";

      (function() {
        let loaded = false;

        function loadScript() {
          if (loaded) return;
          loaded = true;

          const s = document.createElement("script");
          s.src = "https://book.instantroofer.com/js/instant-roofer-google-ads-integration.min.js";
          document.body.appendChild(s);
        }

        // Load after 7 seconds
        const timeoutId = setTimeout(loadScript, 7000);

        // Load on first scroll
        window.addEventListener("scroll", function onScroll() {
          loadScript();
          clearTimeout(timeoutId);
          window.removeEventListener("scroll", onScroll);
        });
      })();
    </script>

title: Changes for Paid seach vendor
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Jordan, Justyna Acosta, Keith Bruss, Predrag
description:
here are the GTM and form changes. After looking at it, it seems like the form changes are prior to the GTM issue, so I made a discussion instead. If we still need to split it, let me know, and I'll make a new one. 

The client's new Paid search vendor sent over these changes that need to be made for his campaign. he sent pretty extensive instructions. Let me know what it will take to make these changes. 

"we’ll be running paid search for TEC, and the tracking on the site right now is missing a few things we need to attribute leads back to ad clicks (GCLID, FBCLID, etc). Rather than patch the existing GTM container (GTM-KZ7BVXXW), I built a new one (GTM-M9R5RS36) with everything wired up properly: Google Ads conversions, GA4 events, CallRail call tracking, Acuity booking attribution, the full pipeline.
Plan is to swap the GTM snippet on the site from the old container to the new one. Before we do that, there’s three small prep items on your end. None of this touches the live site or breaks anything currently running.

1. Gravity Forms hidden fields, every form on the site

Add these 8 hidden fields to each Gravity Form:
  • gclid
  • gbraid
  • wbraid
  • fbclid
  • msclkid
  • utm_source
  • utm_medium
  • utm_campaign
For each:
  • Field type: Hidden
  • Tick "Allow this field to be populated dynamically"
  • Parameter Name = field name, lowercase exact (e.g. gclid)
After you save, send me back the field IDs Gravity Forms assigns. Each field gets an ID, top right of the field settings (looks like ID: 16). Format:
gform_1  gclid field ID: ___
gform_1  fbclid field ID: ___
...etc for every form on the site
Without these, our GTM script can't target the fields to fill them.

2. Acuity internal tracking field

In Acuity admin (owner ID 36347268):
  • Intake Forms, create a new one called Tracking
  • Add one Textbox field: GCLID
  • Tick "This form is for internal use only, don't show it to clients"
  • Assign it to the same appointment types as the main intake form
  • Save
Field gets an ID once you save it. Should show in the URL or field settings, 7 digits. Send that back.

3. LiteSpeed Cache plugin

Server side LiteSpeed is active on the site. Is the LSCache WordPress plugin also installed? If yes, we'll send exclusion rules for swap day so it doesn't strip our scripts.

description:
